name: Airtable
routeName: Personal access token + cURL create record
firstSuccessOutcome: You create a record in an Airtable base and get back its record ID.

steps.action: Open Airtable Web API developer docs
Sign up for a free Airtable account
Verify your email address
Log in to Airtable
Create a base with a table
Open /create/tokens and create new token
Add write scope and base to token
Copy token and base ID
POST create record with cURL bearer token
See new record ID in JSON response

name: Asana
routeName: Personal access token quickstart + cURL create task
firstSuccessOutcome: You create a task in your workspace and get back its task GID.

steps.action: Open Asana developers quick start guide
Sign up for a free Asana account
Verify your email address
Log in to Asana
Open developer console, create new token
Copy your personal access token
Get your workspace GID
POST create task with cURL bearer token
Receive task GID in JSON response

name: DocuSign
routeName: Quickstart tool (auto-configured app sends an envelope)
firstSuccessOutcome: The scaffolded launcher app sends a signing envelope via the eSignature API.

steps.action: Open Docusign Developer Center Quickstart
Create free Docusign developer sandbox account
Activate account via email link
Log in to developer account
Run Quickstart, name app, register integration key
Download Quickstart code in chosen language
Build and run the launcher locally
Log in and grant consent to app
Select example and send an envelope
Confirm envelope sent and signing email arrives

name: Dropbox
routeName: App Console generated access token + cURL list_folder
firstSuccessOutcome: You list the contents of your Dropbox app folder and get JSON entries.

steps.action: Open Dropbox developers getting started guide
Sign up for a free Dropbox account
Verify your email address
Log in and open the App Console
Create a new app in App Console
Generate access token for your account
POST files/list_folder with cURL bearer token
Receive folder entries in JSON response

name: Freshworks
routeName: Freshdesk API key (Basic Auth) + cURL list/create ticket
firstSuccessOutcome: You call the Freshdesk tickets endpoint and get back your tickets as JSON.

steps.action: Open Freshdesk API developer docs
Sign up for a free Freshdesk trial
Verify your email address
Log in to your Freshdesk portal
Open Profile Settings, view API key
GET tickets with cURL Basic Auth apikey:X
Receive tickets JSON response

name: Intercom
routeName: Private app access token + cURL create contact
firstSuccessOutcome: You create a contact in your workspace and get back the contact object.

steps.action: Open Intercom developer getting started guide
Sign up for an Intercom workspace
Verify your email address
Log in and open the Developer Hub
Create a new app on your workspace
Copy access token from Authentication page
POST create contact with cURL bearer token
Receive new contact object in response

name: monday.com
firstSuccessOutcome: You run a GraphQL query and get back your own user id and name.

steps.action: Open monday.com developer getting started docs
Sign up for a free monday.com trial
Verify your email address
Log in to monday.com
Open Developer Center, show and copy API token
POST GraphQL query to api.monday.com/v2
Receive your user id and name

name: Notion
routeName: Personal access token quickstart + cURL create page
firstSuccessOutcome: You create a private page in your workspace and open its returned URL.

steps.action: Open Notion API quickstart
Sign up for a free Notion account
Verify email with confirmation code
Log in to Notion
Open Personal access tokens, click New token
Name token, select workspace, create and copy
Export token as environment variable
POST create page with cURL bearer token
Open returned page URL in browser

name: Pipedrive
routeName: Personal API token (x-api-token) + cURL list deals
firstSuccessOutcome: You call the deals endpoint on your company domain and get JSON back.

steps.action: Open Pipedrive developers getting started docs
Sign up for a free Pipedrive trial
Verify your email address
Log in to Pipedrive
Settings > Personal preferences > API, copy token
Note your company domain
GET deals with x-api-token header via cURL
Receive deals JSON response

name: Zoho
routeName: Self Client (client credentials flow) + Zoho CRM API call
firstSuccessOutcome: You exchange client credentials for an access token and query Zoho CRM data.

steps.action: Open Zoho CRM API docs and API console
Sign up for a free Zoho account
Verify your email address
Log in to Zoho API Console
Create Self Client, copy client ID and secret
POST client credentials to get access token
GET CRM records with Zoho-oauthtoken header
Receive Zoho CRM data in JSON response
